title: Decision Envelope v0.1
description: A canonical governance record for one proposed AI-agent consequence. A decision envelope records what was proposed, how it was classified, what policy/evidence/review state applied, what decision was made, whether execution occurred, and how the record links into a verifiable envelope chain.

schema_version.description: Exact schema version for this decision envelope.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when the decision envelope was created or promoted from a runtime governance event.

runtime_id.description: Identifier for the runtime, gateway, MCP proxy, or governed execution environment that emitted the source governance event.

proposal_id.description: Unique identifier for the proposed consequence being governed.

proposal_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the proposed_action value.

agent_id.description: Identifier for the agent, model wrapper, tool caller, or governed actor that proposed the consequence.

agent_identity_manifest_hash.description: Optional hash of the agent identity manifest that defines the proposing agent's declared identity and authority scope.

proposed_action.description: Exact proposed action, claim, handoff, or consequence-bearing request as received by the governance layer. This field must not be silently rewritten.

normalized_action.description: Deterministically normalized representation of the proposed action used for classification, policy evaluation, hashing, and verification.

normalized_action_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the normalized_action value.

consequence_class.description: Governance class of the proposed consequence.

risk_level.description: Risk level assigned to the proposed consequence.

reversibility.description: Whether the consequence can be undone or meaningfully remediated.

blast_radius.description: Estimated scope of impact if the proposed consequence proceeds.

authority_required.description: Authority level required before the proposed consequence may proceed.

evidence_required.description: Evidence requirement for admitting or authorizing the proposed consequence.

classification_method.description: Method used to classify the proposed consequence.

classification_confidence.description: Confidence assigned to the consequence classification.

policy_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the active policy applied at decision time.

policy_version.description: Version identifier for the active policy applied at decision time.

policy_rule_id.description: Optional identifier of the specific policy rule that determined or contributed to the decision.

decision_engine_version.description: Version of the decision engine or governance runtime that produced the decision.

evidence_manifest_hash.description: Optional hash of the evidence manifest associated with this decision envelope.

evidence_references.description: References to evidence items used in the decision. Required even when empty; an empty array means no evidence references were attached.

state_witness_hash.description: Optional hash of a state witness used to bind approval or decision context to observed system state.

decision.description: Governance decision for the proposed consequence.

review_status.description: Review lifecycle state for the proposed consequence.

reviewer_authority_id.description: Identifier for the reviewer or approving authority. Must be non-null when review_status is APPROVED or REJECTED.

approval_scope.description: Optional scope of reviewer approval, if approval was required.

approval_expiry.description: Optional expiration timestamp for reviewer approval.

execution_boundary.description: Boundary within which execution occurred or was prevented.

execution_status.description: Whether the proposed consequence executed, failed, was blocked, remained pending, or partially executed.

outcome_status.description: Outcome of the governed proposal after decision and execution boundary handling.

outcome_hash.description: Optional SHA-256 hash of the execution result or outcome artifact.

failure_mode.description: Optional failure mode if decision, review, promotion, execution, or verification failed.

tamper_evidence_mode.description: Tamper-evidence mechanism used for this record.

key_id.description: Optional key identifier when tamper_evidence_mode uses keyed authentication such as HMAC_SHA256_V1.

previous_record_hash.description: Hash of the previous decision envelope in the chain. The first envelope uses the bare 64-zero genesis value; subsequent envelopes use the full previous record_hash including the sha256: prefix.

record_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical JSON envelope excluding this record_hash field. Canonical JSON uses sorted keys, compact separators, and ensure_ascii=false.

verifier_version.description: Version of the verifier expected to validate this decision envelope.
